title: Contracts queue overview
content: The Contracts queue (/contracts) holds 11 seeded contracts for the demo. Each row shows filename, counterparty, total contract value (TCV) with thousand separators, source, risk category and score, technical-accounting flags (LEASE / DERIV pills), and processing status. Risk scores drive sort order — High first, then Medium, then Low. Tick the checkbox column to select contracts for batch processing. Click a row to drill into the contract detail.

title: Fixture contracts (canned mode)
content: Eleven canned-mode contracts ship with the prototype: Amazon Web Services (TCV $45M, High risk, ASC 815 derivative flag), Turner Construction ($12.5M, Medium), Accenture LLP ($24M, Medium), Nexus Cloud ($1.35M, High, derivative flag), Summit Builders ($8.5M, Medium), Wieden+Kennedy ($8.4M, Medium), Chubb Commercial Insurance ($15M, Medium), Atlas Realty Partners ($3.6M, Medium, ASC 842 lease flag), Meridian Solutions ($18M, Low), Olympic Insurance ($2.25M, Low, prepaid amortization), TechFlow Consulting ($2.4M, Low). All canned mode runs read from these fixtures.

title: How to process contracts
content: On /contracts, tick the contracts you want and click 'Run selected (N)' to run extract → risk → tech-acct on each. A progress banner tracks 'Processing X of N — Counterparty — extracting…'. When the batch completes, a results modal opens grouped by risk (High / Medium / Low / Failed) with per-row Review and Accrual buttons. After running, click 'Calculate accruals (N)' to compute JEs without submitting; the modal then groups results by routing tier. Click 'Submit all' to send every calculated JE to the JE Review Queue with materiality routing attached.

title: Risk score thresholds (Nike-tuned)
content: Risk thresholds are Nike-scale: TCV >$25M adds 25 points (High bump), TCV >$5M adds 15 points (Medium bump), TCV >$1M adds 5 points (Low bump). Other rule contributions: auto-renewal +10, no liability cap +15, lease component +10, embedded derivative +10. The rules score caps at 60 out of 60. The LLM qualitative signal contributes 0-40 covering uncovered factors (vendor concentration, exclusivity, audit exposure). Combined score 0-100. Categorization: >=75 High, >=40 Medium, <40 Low.

title: Why $25M for High risk (vs $10M)
content: The High-risk TCV threshold was raised from $10M to $25M because Nike's revenue base (~$51B) makes a $10M contract immaterial to financial statements. AS5 / audit performance materiality at Nike scale lands in the hundreds of millions, so a $10M deal shouldn't auto-bump to High. The recalibrated tiers ensure the High category reflects contracts that warrant senior review at Nike's scale. Tunable per tenant — at a mid-market client we'd lower thresholds.

title: JE materiality routing tiers
content: Submitted journal entries route by total amount: <$100K auto-posts (no human review); $100K-$1M routes to Senior Accountant; $1M-$5M routes to Manager; >$5M routes to Manager + Director (dual approval). The router lives at canned-je-store.routeByMateriality and the equivalent server-side at je-materiality.routeForApproval. Approval triggers the Posting Agent which calls BlackLine -> SAP BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST. Below-threshold entries skip the queue and pick up SAP doc numbers immediately.

title: Dual approval (>$5M) — current implementation
content: JEs over $5M show 'Manager + Director (Dual)' as their materiality routing label. In the current prototype this is label-only — a single Approve click still posts. Real two-signature gating with separate manager-approved and director-approved states is roadmap. The label communicates the policy; the mechanics are intentionally simple for the demo. To implement: add two boolean approval flags on proposed_journal_entries, post only when both are true, render two distinct sign-off buttons on the JE Review Queue row.

title: Auto-reversal policy
content: Accrual reversals are policy — they inherit approval from the original journal entry and post automatically on the reversal_date. No separate human sign-off required. This matches SAP F.81 / BlackLine production behavior: reversals are mechanical undo entries (same lines flipped), dated next period, run as a batch. The audit trail records both SAP doc numbers (original posting + reversal) under the original approver's user_id. In canned mode, the queue refresh sweeps posted JEs whose reversal_date has arrived and flips them to 'reversed'. A presenter '(Demo) advance clock' button force-flips for live demos.

title: When reversals should NOT auto-post (exception gates)
content: Recommended production exception gates before auto-reversing: (1) original posting status must be 'posted' (not rejected/voided); (2) contract still active (not terminated mid-period); (3) reversal period is open (not hard-closed); (4) no material change to the underlying estimate (e.g., new invoice arrived showing >10% variance). If any gate fails, route to an exception queue for manual Manager review instead of auto-posting. The current prototype implements gate (1) only — period-close gates and variance gates are roadmap.

title: ASC 842 lease flag
content: ASC 842 lease accounting applies when a contract gives Nike the right to control the use of an identified asset for a period of time in exchange for consideration. NOAH flags this if the contract has a lease_component attribute set true. Flagged contracts route to senior tech-acct review for ROU asset / lease liability classification. Currently flagged contracts: Atlas Realty Partners (office lease).

title: ASC 815 embedded derivative flag
content: ASC 815 embedded-derivative review is triggered when a contract contains pricing or settlement terms indexed to a financial variable (FX, commodity, equity, interest rate). NOAH flags this if the embedded_derivative attribute is true. Flagged contracts require bifurcation analysis. Currently flagged contracts: Amazon Web Services (cloud-credits indexing), Nexus Cloud Inc.

title: Close cycle — five phases
content: The Close Cockpit models five phases: Phase 1 Pre-Close (Day -1 and before) scans SAP for open items, predicts at-risk accounts, produces a readiness report. Phase 2 Execute (Day 1-3) triggers BlackLine Smart Close, monitors via Event Grid, routes exceptions. Phase 3 Consolidate (Day 4) rolls up subsidiary entries, posts eliminations and FX translation, locks group totals. Phase 4 Validate (Day 5-6) runs balance validation, recon agent checks, drafts narrative. Phase 5 Gate (Day 6) confirms exceptions are below materiality, captures controller sign-off, closes the period.

title: Variance Analysis tab — three views
content: /narrative has three tabs. P&L Variance Analysis draws line-item commentary on each P&L row using Claude (or Qwen in local mode); deterministic generation, ~2-3 sentences per line, CFO-memo tone. B/S Variance Analysis shows Period End vs Prior Year End for 13 balance-sheet line items including a contract-driven Accrued Liabilities row that updates live as JEs are submitted on /contracts. Executive Summary drafts a board-ready close summary using top-3 variances + close metrics from the Cockpit (or a 5.2-day baseline if Cockpit idle). Tab order: P&L Variance Analysis, B/S Variance Analysis, Executive Summary.

title: B/S Variance Analysis — Accrued Liabilities is contract-driven
content: On /narrative -> B/S Variance Analysis, the Accrued Liabilities row is special: its current period balance equals a seeded BAU base ($5,950M) PLUS the live sum of credits to GL 2310 from JEs submitted on /contracts. Variance and percent are recomputed from that live total. Clicking Generate on that row produces a deterministic commentary that names contributing contracts, materiality routing breakdown, scheduled auto-reversals, and ASC 842/815 flags from the contracts. Other rows use seeded driver text + auto-flag lines with >10% YoY variance for disclosure consideration.

title: JE Review Queue layout
content: The JE Review Queue (/review) splits JEs into two sections: Pending Review at the top (status='submitted', with Approve/Reject actions) and Recent Activity below (posted/reversed/rejected, read-only with status pills). Each row shows Company Code (1000 = Nike US), debit account number with name, debit amount, credit account number, credit amount — what an SAP reviewer actually checks. Click the chevron to expand a row in place: full multi-line JE table, lifecycle panel (Submitted / Approved / Posted / Reversal), and the calculation narrative with a Download button for an audit-ready tab-delimited worksheet.

title: JE Review Queue header buttons
content: /review header has two buttons: '(Demo) advance clock' force-flips any posted JE with a future reversal_date to Reversed status (presenter aid for live demos; production handles this automatically on the calendar reversal_date). 'Refresh' re-fetches the queue from the JE store (canned) or Postgres (live) and runs the date-gated reversal sweep. The flash banner under the title confirms what happened on each click.

title: 27 contract attributes extracted
content: The Extractor agent pulls 27 structured attributes per contract: counterparty, contract_type, effective_date, expiration_date, total_contract_value, currency, payment_terms, billing_frequency, fee_schedule, service_description, service_start_date, service_end_date, auto_renewal, termination_notice_days, governing_law, indemnification_present, liability_cap, lease_component, embedded_derivative, expense_recognition_method, performance_obligations, pricing_variability, minimum_commitment, exclusivity_clause, ip_ownership, confidentiality_term, change_order_mechanism. Each attribute returns value + confidence (0-1) + source_page. Confidence <0.5 highlights amber in the AttributeChecklist.

title: Demo flow end-to-end
content: Recommended demo path (~30 min): 1) /contracts — Run selected to extract+score 11 contracts, then Calculate accruals -> Submit all to send JEs to JE Review Queue. 2) /review — expand a JE, show line detail + Download calc worksheet, Approve a JE, click (Demo) advance clock to flip a reversal. 3) /narrative — show Variance Commentary, Executive Summary, then Balance Sheet (note Accrued Liabilities is contract-driven). 4) /copilot — try the suggestion chips, demonstrate live answers update with state. 5) / (Close Cockpit) — Start the simulation, watch phases animate. Cockpit is shown LAST — it's the orchestration frame, not the entry point.

title: Architecture — live mode stack
content: Live mode stack: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector on port 5434 (docker-compose), Express.js server on port 3001 with routes for contracts/metabase/search/audit/je/kb. Ollama at localhost:11434 with Qwen 7B for chat completion and nomic-embed-text (768 dims) for embeddings. Vite dev server on 5173 (or first free port). Production target: Anthropic API on Azure AI Foundry with managed agents, BlackLine for certification workflows, SAP for posting via BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST. Canned mode (VITE_MODE=canned) runs without backend — fixtures in fixtures/metabase.json + in-memory JE store.

title: Live mode vs canned mode
content: Canned mode (VITE_MODE=canned, the demo default) runs entirely in the browser using fixtures and an in-memory JE store. No Postgres, no Ollama. Predictable timing — 1-2 seconds of synthetic dwell per agent step. Live mode (default pnpm dev) hits the Express server which calls Postgres + Ollama. Extraction takes 30-90s per contract on Qwen 7B (CPU). Switch via the .env file VITE_MODE setting. Both modes share the same UI components and data shapes — only the data sources change.

title: Audit trail
content: Every state transition pushes an audit event: je_submit, je_approve, je_post, je_reject, je_reversal. In live mode these write to the audit_events table with id, ts, event_type, contract_id, agent (which agent emitted), confidence, payload (JSONB), user_id. In canned mode the events live in an in-memory array keyed on globalThis to survive Vite HMR. The audit screen at /contracts/:id/audit renders this timeline. Reversals record under the original approver's user_id, not 'auto-reversed', preserving accountability.

title: Accrual pipeline
content: calculateAccrual takes contract attributes + full text + period end and produces inputs (parsed accrual inputs), calc (deterministic math result), je (proposed journal entry), missing (any gaps). The math is pure functions in lib/accrual-math.ts — no LLM in the loop for the actual accrual calculation; only the input-parsing stage uses Claude. Methods supported: straight-line, prepaid-amortization, percent-of-completion. AccrualGapError throws when required inputs are missing (service_start_date, fee_schedule, etc.) — surfaces in the UI as a yellow 'Cannot compute' panel listing the specific missing fields.

title: Prepaid amortization vs accrual
content: Two different JE patterns. An accrual recognizes expense in the current period before the invoice arrives — DR Expense, CR Accrued Liabilities — and reverses next period when the actual invoice posts. A prepaid amortization recognizes ratable expense over time on a payment already made — DR Expense, CR Prepaid Asset — and does NOT reverse. Olympic Insurance is the prepaid example in fixtures (annual premium paid up-front). Its proposed_je has reversal_date = null and the JECard component reads 'Amortization · no reversal — prepaid drawdown' for that case.

title: Changing the GL account on a proposed accrual JE
content: Default GL accounts on the JE builder are 6810 Services Expense (debit) and 2310 Accrued Liabilities (credit). Today the prototype hard-codes those defaults in lib/je-builder.ts; the function already accepts expenseAccount and liabilityAccount overrides as optional parameters but no UI surfaces them. To customize, three options. (1) Reviewer override before submit: add inline-edit fields to the AccrualProposal screen so the Senior Accountant can swap the account number on the proposed JE before clicking Submit for Review — small UI change, no schema change. (2) Per-tenant chart-of-accounts mapping table: a coa_mapping table keyed on (tenant, contract_type, service_description) returning the right expense + liability accounts so Nike's CoA conventions apply automatically per contract category; mapping resolved during accrual calculation. (3) Both: mapping picks the default, reviewer can still override. Recommended path is (3). Audit log records the override + reason if the reviewer changes the account.

title: Submit all month-end JEs together (batch flow)
content: Yes — submitting JEs one contract at a time is NOT the workflow. Once a contract has been through extract → risk → tech-acct (HITL review on /contracts), the batch flow takes over. On /contracts: tick the contracts you want to accrue (the header checkbox toggles all), click 'Calculate accruals (N)'. NOAH computes a JE for each in sequence; a results modal opens grouped by where each JE will route (Manager + Director dual >$5M, Manager $1M-$5M, Senior Accountant $100K-$1M, auto-post <$100K). At this point nothing has been submitted — review the routing and amounts. Click 'Submit all (N)' to push every calculated JE to the JE Review Queue with materiality routing already attached. Sub-$100K entries auto-post immediately to SAP and pick up doc numbers; larger entries land in /review for approval. One click submits the full month-end batch.

title: SG&A expense recorded this period
content: SG&A (Selling, General & Administrative) expense recorded this period: $1,745,000,000 ($1.7B) vs prior period $1,692,000,000 ($1.7B), a +$53M (+3.1%) YoY increase. Driver: corporate headcount investment ~+4%, FX-neutral wage inflation ~3%. Entity split (current period): NA $820M, EMEA $460M, GC $285M, APLA $180M. SG&A sits inside the Opex bucket alongside Demand Creation (Marketing) $1.180B, R&D $178M, Store Operations $412M, D&A $214M. Total Opex this period: $3.729B. Contract-driven accruals from /contracts post to GL 6810 (Services Expense) which rolls up into SG&A; the 11 reviewed contracts represent the services/professional-fees portion of the SG&A line. Source: seedPnL.ts, line opex-sga.

title: How many contracts/JEs require which review tier
content: JEs route by total amount, not by contract: <$100K auto-posts (no human review); $100K-$1M routes to Senior Accountant; $1M-$5M routes to Manager; >$5M routes to Manager + Director (dual approval). Whether a contract requires Manager review depends on its accrual amount, not its TCV — a $50M contract paid quarterly might book a $4M accrual (Manager tier), while a $2M contract paid annually books $167K (Senior Accountant tier). To see the actual tier breakdown for the current period, calculate accruals on /contracts; the post-calc modal shows per-tier counts and totals. Once submitted, the JE Review Queue shows pending JEs grouped by tier.

title: Total revenue + gross margin (Q2 FY26)
content: Total revenue this period: $7,107M ($7.1B) vs prior period $7,133M — essentially flat at -$26M (-0.4%). Three revenue lines roll up: Direct-to-Consumer $2,845M (+$135M / +5.0%), Wholesale $4,120M (-$175M / -4.1% on NA key-account destocking), Licensing & Royalty $142M (+$14M / +10.9% on Greater China licensee expansion). Total COGS this period: $4,225M ($3,840M product + $385M logistics). Gross profit: $2,882M; gross margin 40.6% versus 42.0% prior — about 140 bps of compression driven by mix shift to premium and freight headwinds offset by ocean rate relief.

title: Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) revenue
content: DTC revenue this period: $2,845M ($2.8B) vs prior $2,710M — a $135M (+5.0%) YoY increase. Driver: volume +3%, price +2%, mix +0.5%, FX headwind -0.5%. Entity split (current period): NA $1,280M, EMEA $720M, GC $515M, APLA $330M. DTC continues to outperform wholesale this period — the +5% DTC growth offsets the -4.1% wholesale decline on the top line. This is the largest single-line YoY positive variance and lands in the top-3 dollar movers used by the Executive Summary draft.

title: Wholesale revenue
content: Wholesale revenue this period: $4,120M ($4.1B) vs prior $4,295M — a -$175M (-4.1%) YoY decline. Driver: volume -2%, key-account destocking in NA, EMEA resilient. Entity split (current period): NA $1,740M, EMEA $1,190M, GC $750M, APLA $440M. Wholesale is the largest single revenue line and the largest YoY dollar decline this period. Land this carefully in narrative: the wholesale weakness is mostly NA-specific; EMEA and APLA grew. This line is a top-3 variance on the Executive Summary.

title: Licensing & Royalty revenue
content: Licensing & Royalty revenue this period: $142M vs prior $128M — a +$14M (+10.9%) YoY increase. Smallest revenue line but highest growth rate. Driver: new licensee expansion in Greater China, royalty rate mix favorable. Entity split (current period): NA $42M, EMEA $35M, GC $48M, APLA $17M. GC is the largest entity for this line, reflecting the licensee expansion.

title: COGS — Product + Logistics
content: Total COGS this period: $4,225M (Product $3,840M + Logistics & Distribution $385M). Product COGS $3,840M vs prior $3,715M — a +$125M (+3.4%) YoY increase. Driver: material deflation -1.5% offset by higher freight rates and mix shift to premium product. Logistics & Distribution $385M vs prior $420M — a -$35M (-8.3%) decrease driven by reduced ocean freight rates and improved EMEA DC throughput. Net: COGS +$90M (+2.2%) on essentially flat revenue produces gross-margin compression.

title: Demand Creation (Marketing) spend
content: Demand Creation (Marketing) spend this period: $1,180M ($1.2B) vs prior $1,050M — a +$130M (+12.4%) YoY increase. Driver: campaign investment for Q2 product launch, digital spend +18% YoY. Entity split: NA $485M, EMEA $340M, GC $220M, APLA $135M. This is one of the top-3 dollar movers and a flagged risk on the Executive Summary — spend ran ahead of plan and ROAS validation is recommended before Q3 commitments. Sits inside the Opex bucket alongside SG&A, R&D, Store Operations, and D&A.

title: R&D — Research, Design & Development
content: R&D (Research, Design & Development) spend this period: $178M vs prior $162M — a +$16M (+9.9%) YoY increase. Driver: innovation center expansion, new materials science initiative. Entity split: NA $102M, EMEA $38M, GC $24M, APLA $14M. NA-concentrated; reflects HQ-led innovation programs.

title: Store Operations expense
content: Store Operations expense this period: $412M vs prior $395M — a +$17M (+4.3%) YoY increase. Driver: lease expense +4% from renewals, labor +3% at minimum-wage locations. Entity split: NA $168M, EMEA $112M, GC $95M, APLA $37M. Note: the lease component flows through both Store Ops (P&L expense) and the Operating Lease ROU + Liability lines on the balance sheet — Atlas Realty Partners is the contract example in the reviewed portfolio.

title: Depreciation & Amortization (D&A)
content: D&A (Depreciation & Amortization) this period: $214M vs prior $198M — a +$16M (+8.1%) YoY increase. Driver: tech modernization capex coming online, DC automation rollouts. Entity split: NA $92M, EMEA $58M, GC $42M, APLA $22M. Non-cash expense; the increase reflects PP&E additions over the past 12 months (PP&E grew from $4.94B to $5.14B per the balance sheet).

title: Below-line items — interest, other income, tax
content: Below-line items: Net Interest Expense $48M (+$13M / +37.1% YoY) on higher short-term borrowings and elevated rate environment. Other Income/(Expense) -$22M vs +$18M prior — a -$40M swing (-222.2%) driven by FX translation losses on APLA exposure plus a prior-period gain on asset sale that didn't repeat. Income Tax Expense $228M vs $242M prior — a -$14M (-5.8%) decrease. Effective tax rate 20.8% vs 21.6% prior on discrete benefits in EMEA. The Other Income swing is the single largest YoY dollar move in below-line items and is flagged on the Executive Summary.

title: Current Assets — Cash, AR, Inventory, Prepaid
content: Current assets at period end: Cash & Equivalents $10.5B (-$200M / -1.9% on share repurchases and dividends offsetting strong operating cash); Accounts Receivable $5.1B (+$200M / +4.1%, DSO flat at 38 days); Inventories $7.5B (-$700M / -8.5% — successful destocking program, channel inventory normalized); Prepaid Expenses & Other $1.4B (+$100M / +7.6% on annual insurance premium prepayment plus higher cloud-services prepaid — Olympic Insurance is the prepaid example in the reviewed contract portfolio). Inventory destocking is the largest favorable working-capital movement.

title: Non-Current Assets — PP&E, ROU, Goodwill
content: Non-current assets at period end: Property, Plant & Equipment (net) $5.1B (+$200M / +4.1%, DC automation capex + new innovation center, net of D&A); Operating Lease ROU Assets $3.2B (+$200M / +6.6%, renewals at higher rates plus new flagship leases in EMEA + GC — Atlas Realty Partners is the reviewed-contract example); Goodwill, Intangibles & Other LT Assets $2.0B (+$140M / +7.7%, deferred tax asset growth + minor brand acquisition in Q1).

title: Current Liabilities — AP, Income Tax Payable
content: Current liabilities at period end: Accounts Payable $2.5B (-$200M / -7.4%, supplier payment-terms standardization plus strategic AP financing); Accrued Liabilities $5.95B (+$150M / +2.6%) — this is the contract-driven row, see kb-narrative-balance-sheet; Income Taxes Payable $520M (+$105M / +25.3% on higher pre-tax income plus APLA discrete items). Accrued Liabilities is the only line on the balance sheet that's recomputed live from the JE Review Queue — the seeded BAU base of $5,800M plus the live sum of GL 2310 credits from submitted JEs equals the displayed current period balance.

title: Non-Current Liabilities — Long-Term Debt, Lease Liabilities
content: Non-current liabilities at period end: Long-Term Debt $8.9B (+$20M / +0.2%, no new issuances; minor mark-to-market on hedged tranches); Operating Lease Liabilities (LT) $2.7B (+$100M / +3.8%, mirrors the ROU asset growth, renewal pipeline in EMEA). The lease liability balance is paired with the ROU asset on the asset side — they grow together as new leases are signed.

title: Total Shareholders' Equity
content: Total Shareholders' Equity at period end: $23.2B vs prior $20.3B — a +$2.9B (+14.3%) YoY increase. Driver: net income plus other comprehensive income, partially offset by share buybacks and dividends. This is the largest single-line absolute movement on the balance sheet this period and is a one-line reflection of profit retention plus market-driven OCI.

title: AWS Enterprise contract
content: Amazon Web Services, Inc. — TCV $45M, High risk (score 85). Contract type: enterprise cloud services. Auto-renewal: yes. Liability cap: present. Embedded derivative: yes — pricing escalator indexed to CPI-U triggers ASC 815 review. Contract spans multiple regions, with data-sovereignty obligations and a minimum-commitment structure with true-up. Risk score factors: TCV >$25M (+25), auto-renewal (+10), embedded derivative (+10) — plus LLM-flagged factors including the CPI escalator, multi-region data-sovereignty span, and minimum-commitment with true-up. Largest contract by TCV in the portfolio. ASC 815 derivative review required. File: Contract_5_AWS_Enterprise.docx.

title: Accenture LLP contract
content: Accenture LLP — TCV $24M, Medium risk (score 68). Contract type: Managed Services Agreement (Finance back-office BPO — AP processing, AR collections, close support per defined SLAs). Term: 2026-01-01 through 2028-12-31 (36 months). Fee schedule: $667K monthly base + T&M overages. Auto-renewal: yes. Liability cap: NO (notable risk factor). Minimum commitment: $8M annual floor. Period accrual: $667K straight-line monthly. Risk reasons: TCV >$5M, auto-renewal, no liability cap, vendor concentration — top finance BPO provider. File: Contract_2_Professional_Services_Outsourcing.docx.

title: Turner Construction contract
content: Turner Construction — TCV $12.5M, Medium risk (score 72). Contract type: construction services for retail remodel. Auto-renewal: no. Liability cap: NO. Risk reasons include the no-liability-cap exposure plus construction-specific factors. The Summit Builders contract ($8.5M, retail buildout) is structurally similar with a milestone-certified accrual. Both are construction contracts that book to GL 1810 (Construction in Progress) rather than to accrued liabilities — they are CIP capitalizations, not period-end accruals. File: Contract_4_Construction_Retail_Remodel.docx.

title: Nexus Cloud SaaS Renewal contract
content: Nexus Cloud Inc. — TCV $1.35M, High risk (score 71) despite small TCV. Contract type: SaaS Renewal. Auto-renewal: yes. Liability cap: limited (prior 12 months fees, with IP/confidentiality carve-outs). Embedded derivative: yes — flagged for ASC 815 review. Why the High score on a small-TCV deal: the qualitative LLM signal flagged enough concerning factors (auto-renewal, derivative, carve-out structure) to push it over the threshold. Demonstrates that risk is not a TCV-only function. File: Golden_B_Nexus_SaaS_Renewal.docx.

title: Atlas Realty office lease (ASC 842)
content: Atlas Realty Partners, LP — TCV $3.6M, Medium risk (score 58). Contract type: multi-year office lease. Lease component: YES — flagged for ASC 842 lease review. Liability cap: limited (landlord liability limited to interest in Building). Auto-renewal: no. The lease creates an ROU asset and a corresponding lease liability on the balance sheet rather than a period accrual. The reviewed contract is the demonstration example for ASC 842 treatment in the prototype. File: Golden_C_Atlas_Office_Lease.docx.

title: Olympic Insurance — prepaid amortization (not accrual)
content: Olympic Insurance Group, Inc. — TCV $2.25M, Low risk (score 32). Multi-year P&C insurance with annual premium prepaid up-front. The proposed JE is a prepaid amortization (DR Insurance Expense $62.5K, CR Prepaid Insurance $62.5K), NOT an accrual: there is no reversal — reversal_date is null. The JE Card explicitly reads 'Amortization · no reversal — prepaid drawdown' for this case. Year-1 annual premium $750K paid 2026-01-01, amortized $62.5K/month (4 months elapsed = $250K cumulative through period end). Demonstrates the prepaid-vs-accrual distinction. File: Golden_E_Olympic_Insurance_MultiYear.docx.

title: Other reviewed contracts
content: Remaining reviewed contracts in the portfolio: Wieden+Kennedy LLC $8.4M Medium (62) — advertising campaign services, no liability cap. Chubb Commercial Insurance $15M Medium (48) — multi-year property & casualty insurance, prepaid premium component. Summit Builders LLC $8.5M Medium (65) — construction milestone certification, books to GL 1810 (CIP). Meridian Solutions Inc. $18M Low (28) — advisory services, liability cap (2x prior 12mo fees), 100% extraction confidence (golden reference document). TechFlow Consulting LLC $2.4M Low (24) — Master Services Agreement, smallest TCV in the portfolio. All 11 contracts in the portfolio are accessible from /contracts.

title: Entities in scope (NA, EMEA, GC, APLA, Corporate, Global)
content: Six entities run in parallel through the close cycle: NA (North America — leads, currently in Execute), EMEA (Europe Middle East Africa), GC (Greater China), APLA (Asia Pacific Latin America), Corporate, Global. The cockpit visualizes entity progression — NA leads, EMEA next, GC and APLA queued, Corporate and Global fold in at consolidation. P&L reporting splits revenue and major Opex lines across the four operating regions (NA, EMEA, GC, APLA); Corporate and Global handle eliminations + consolidation entries. Today the agentic prototype is scoped to NA / Global only — EMEA, GC, APLA are architecturally blocked under the current model and are part of the expansion roadmap.

title: How to approve a JE
content: Approving a JE: go to /review (JE Review Queue) → find the JE in the Pending review section → click Approve. Single click. NOAH's Posting Agent calls BlackLine which calls SAP BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST. Original entry posts immediately with a fresh SAP doc number, visible in the lifecycle panel. Reviewer title is auto-derived from the materiality tier — Senior Accountant for $100K-$1M, Manager for $1M-$5M, Manager + Director for >$5M. The audit trail records under that title. If the JE has a reversal_date, that reversal is scheduled (auto-posts on the reversal date, no separate sign-off). Optional: click the chevron first to expand the row and see the full multi-line JE table + lifecycle + supporting calculation before approving.

title: How to reject a JE
content: Rejecting a JE: go to /review (JE Review Queue) → find the JE → click Reject. An inline reason input appears (required) → enter the rejection reason → click Send. JE moves to Rejected status with the reason and rejecter name in the audit trail. The Senior Accountant who prepared the JE sees the rejection and can recalculate with corrected inputs. Common rejection reasons: incorrect GL account, missing supporting documentation, period-mismatch, accrual not yet earned, contract terminated. Rejecter title is auto-derived from the materiality tier (same logic as approval).

title: How to run extraction (or any agent step) on a contract
content: Three ways to run extraction: (1) Batch on /contracts — tick the contracts you want and click 'Run selected (N)' to run extract → risk → tech-acct in sequence. (2) Single contract — click into the contract from /contracts, then click 'Run full chain' on the contract detail page. (3) Single step — on the contract detail page, click 'Risk only' or 'Tech-Acct only' if attributes are already extracted. In canned mode each step has a synthetic dwell of about a second. In live mode each step calls Claude (or Qwen via Ollama in local mode); extraction takes 30-90s per contract on Qwen 7B CPU.

title: How to upload a new contract
content: Uploading a new contract: drop a .pdf or .docx into the upload zone on /contracts (or click to choose files). Multiple files can be uploaded together. The server extracts text, generates a vector embedding via nomic-embed-text (768 dims), and stores the contract in the contract_metabase table. New contracts appear in the queue with empty agent_status — run extract → risk → tech-acct via Run selected to populate. NOTE: upload is disabled in canned mode (the demo runs on fixtures); in live mode, the server route POST /api/contracts handles ingestion.

title: Export options — JE worksheet, exec summary slide, copy buttons
content: Available exports today: (1) JE calculation worksheet — expand any JE row in /review and click 'Download'. Tab-delimited .txt file with the full JE body, lifecycle metadata, and the supporting calculation reasoning. Drop into BlackLine work papers. (2) Executive Summary slide — on /narrative → Executive Summary, click 'Create slide' to open a 16:9 preview, then 'Save as PDF / Print' to print just the slide. (3) Copy buttons on Variance Commentary, Executive Summary, and B/S Variance commentary panels copy the formatted text to clipboard for paste into Word/Outlook/Teams. PNG/PPTX export is roadmap (would require html-to-image or pptxgenjs library).

title: SOX compliance + controls
content: SOX-compliant design baked into the prototype: (1) Segregation of duties — Senior Accountant prepares JEs, Manager/Director approves, Posting Agent posts. The same user cannot prepare and approve. (2) Materiality routing — automatic per-JE based on amount (<$100K auto-post, $100K-$1M Senior Accountant, $1M-$5M Manager, >$5M dual approval) so no JE bypasses appropriate review. (3) Audit trail — every state transition (je_submit, je_approve, je_post, je_reject, je_reversal) writes to audit_events with timestamp, user_id, agent_id, confidence, and full payload. (4) Inherited approval for reversals — the original approval covers the reversal under the same user_id, preserving accountability. (5) Reviewer title derived from tier — audit log records the actual approver role, not a hardcoded value. Production engagement includes governance framework with full audit trail per the 12-week PoV scope.

title: AI models — Claude (production), Qwen (local), nomic embeddings
content: Models in use: Claude (Anthropic API on Azure AI Foundry) is the production target for chat completion — extraction, risk LLM signal, narrative drafting. Qwen 2.5 7B via Ollama is the local-eval option for offline development and IT-restricted environments; takes 30-90s per contract on CPU. nomic-embed-text via Ollama produces 768-dim vector embeddings for the contract semantic-search index and the noah_kb knowledge base. Both Claude and Qwen are configured via system prompts that explicitly forbid number fabrication; JSON schema validation rejects malformed output. In canned mode (the demo default), agent calls return pre-baked fixtures with synthetic dwell — no model is actually invoked.

title: Performance — extraction time + scale
content: Performance characteristics: Canned mode — 1-2s synthetic dwell per agent step, ~5s for full chain per contract, ~55s for batch of 11 contracts sequentially. Live mode on Qwen 7B CPU — 30-90s per extract step, 30-60s per risk step, 30-60s per tech-acct step. ~3-5 minutes per contract end-to-end. Production target on Anthropic API + Foundry GPU — sub-30s per contract end-to-end with parallelization (concurrent calls per contract, configurable concurrency limit per tenant). Scaling notes: HNSW vector index handles 100K+ embeddings without degradation; pgvector cosine similarity is sub-100ms for typical semantic searches. The architectural scaling-curve change is the value: AI-native extraction cost approaches zero as contract volume rises, breaking the linear-FTE-scaling regime of manual review.

title: Database schema — Postgres + pgvector
content: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension on port 5434 (docker-compose). Core tables: contracts (blob storage, sha256 dedup); contract_metabase (27 attributes JSONB, risk score/category, tech-acct flags, full text, vector(768) embedding HNSW-indexed for cosine similarity); proposed_journal_entries (JE lifecycle with status, materiality_tier, posting_ref, reversal_date, reversed_at); audit_events (SOX traceability — event_type, contract_id, agent, confidence, payload JSONB, user_id, ts); pnl_lines (seed P&L for narrative); noah_kb (knowledge base — content TEXT, embedding VECTOR(768), title, topic, keywords TEXT[]). Init script in docker/init.sql. Idempotent migrations applied at server boot via server/src/lib/migrations.ts.

title: 12-week PoV (proof of value) scope
content: The ask in the deck: greenlight a $0 PoV (proof of value) — 12 weeks, 3 production agents, deployed in Nike's environment. Immediate next step is a 2-week discovery workshop. What Nike provides: SAP read access (CDS views for GL/AP/AR/FA), AWS Bedrock workspace, 2-3 part-time finance SMEs (Controller rep, AP/AR lead, IT liaison), VP-level steering sponsor for bi-weekly reviews, current SOX control matrix and testing methodology. What Deloitte delivers: 2-week Discovery Workshop, three production agents (Reconciliation, JE Anomaly, GL) deployed and operational, NOAH Orchestrator as the connecting layer between agents and SAP, SOX-compliant agent governance with full audit trail, phased plan to scale from 3 agents to 23 agents over 12-18 months. $0 cost to Nike — Deloitte absorbs the engagement risk.

title: Scaling plan — 3 agents to 23, global expansion
content: Post-PoV scaling plan: phased expansion from 3 production agents (Reconciliation, JE Anomaly, GL — proven during the PoV) to 23 agents over 12-18 months. Includes: AI Contract Review (Document Intelligence, ASC 840/842/815), Accrual Calc & JE Prep, Consolidation & IC, Reporting & Analysis (variance commentary, flux, dashboards), Close Orchestrator. Geographic expansion follows: today scoped to NA / Global; phased rollout to EMEA, Greater China, APLA, Converse over the same 12-18 months. The architectural scaling — agents scale on demand without proportional headcount — is what enables global expansion in the first place. Linear FTE scaling has been the ceiling that blocked these geographies under the current model.

title: Create Slide — exec summary as a 16:9 deck slide
content: On /narrative → Executive Summary, after generating the close narrative, click 'Create slide' to open a 16:9 slide preview modal. Layout: brand-accent left rail, dark navy header band with eyebrow + date, large headline (the generated hook sentence), two-column body (Key Highlights with brand-accent ▸ bullets and Risks in amber column), Recommendation footer with brand-accent rule, Deloitte / Nike Controlling brand footer. Toolbar above the slide: 'Save as PDF / Print' triggers a print stylesheet that hides everything except the slide and forces landscape orientation, so the user gets a single 16:9 PDF page. Close button or Esc closes the modal. PNG/PPTX export is roadmap.

title: Contract selection model + Run all / Calculate accruals / Submit all
content: Contracts queue (/contracts) supports per-row + select-all checkboxes (header checkbox is tri-state: indeterminate when partially selected). Default on first load: all selected. Subsequent refreshes preserve selection. Two batch actions in the header drive off the same selection: 'Run selected (N)' runs extract → risk → tech-acct on each selected contract; 'Calculate accruals (N)' computes a JE per selected contract and opens a results modal grouped by routing tier. From the calculate-accruals modal, 'Submit all' sends every calculated JE to the JE Review Queue with materiality routing pre-attached. Sub-$100K entries auto-post immediately; larger entries land in /review. Clicking a row navigates to the contract detail; clicking a checkbox stops propagation and only toggles selection.

title: How NOAH Help loads — KB sources vs. live data
content: NOAH Help mixes two data sources at query time. STATIC knowledge base (loaded once at app start): ~50 chunks defined in fixtures/kb-chunks.json — definitions, policies, architecture, feature descriptions, contract metadata, P&L/B/S line definitions, workflows, SOX/audit, model and performance specs, the 12-week PoV scope. In live mode these are seeded to the noah_kb Postgres table with nomic-embed-text 768-dim vectors and searched via pgvector cosine similarity. In canned mode (the demo default) the same chunks are searched via BM25-style keyword scoring. LIVE state (computed on every query): contract counts and risk distribution from listContracts(); JE queue state, materiality tier breakdown, accrual totals from listAllJEs(); P&L line values from seedPnL; B/S line values from seedBalanceSheet; close phase from useCloseStore. Live answers are computed by intent matchers (kb-agent.ts) that run BEFORE KB retrieval — these are the answers that update as you click through the demo. To get a non-zero accrual figure, you must run the workflow: /contracts → tick → Calculate accruals → Submit all. The KB itself does NOT store live amounts; that's intentional so the KB doesn't go stale. To extend the KB, add chunks to fixtures/kb-chunks.json (canned + live both read it) and re-seed Postgres via `cd server && pnpm seed:kb` for live mode.

title: (Demo) Advance clock — fast-forward auto-reversals
content: On /review (JE Review Queue), the '(Demo) advance clock' button is a presenter aid only — explicitly labeled (Demo). In production the calendar drives reversal posting on the reversal_date. For the demo, the button force-flips any posted JE with a future reversal_date to Reversed status with a fresh SAP doc, simulating what the calendar would do automatically. The flash banner under the title confirms what happened: '{N} JEs auto-reversed' or 'No posted JEs with a scheduled reversal_date to advance' (Olympic prepaid amortization and Summit Builders CIP have null reversal_dates and are not eligible).
